name: ofa-vscode
description: One-click SLURM + ofa connection for VS Code Chat on Kestrel and other HPC systems.

ofa.kestrelHostnamePattern.markdownDescription: Regex used to confirm that a Remote-SSH host is a Kestrel node. If your site uses different hostnames, override this so `OFA: Connect` still recognizes it. Matched against the output of `uname -n` on the remote.

ofa.slurm.account.markdownDescription: Slurm account for the `salloc` request. Empty string = auto-detect via `sacctmgr` (same behaviour as `bin/ofa`).

ofa.slurm.walltime.markdownDescription: Wall time for the `salloc` request. Format: `HH:MM:SS`.

ofa.slurm.gres.markdownDescription: Slurm GRES for the `salloc` request. Set to `gpu:h100:1` or similar on sites with typed GPU GRES.

ofa.ofaBinPath.markdownDescription: Absolute path to the `ofa` executable. Leave empty to auto-detect (checks `$OFA_ROOT/bin/ofa`, then `bash -lic 'command -v ofa'`, then the Kestrel deploy default `/nopt/nrel/apps/cpu_stack/software/openfoam/assistant/bin/ofa`). Set explicitly on non-Kestrel installs or if your `module load assistant` is not sourced by an interactive login shell.

enumDescriptions: (default) Do not set OFA_MODEL. bin/ofa picks its own default (currently gemma4:31b) — the only model with validated safety guards (TESTED_MODELS).
Google Gemma 4 — 31B, ~19 GB (Q4_K_M). Default; the only model in TESTED_MODELS. Sampling: T=1.0, top_p=0.95.
Google Gemma 4 — 31B Q8_0 (~34 GB). Higher precision than Q4_K_M default, ~46 GB free for KV cache on H100 — pick this when long context or better fidelity matters. Same base weights as gemma4:31b.
Google Gemma 4 — 26B, smaller sibling of the default. Same sampling.
Meta Llama 4 Scout — 108B MoE. Long context (capped at 128K). Sampling: T=0.6, top_p=0.9.
Meta Llama 3.3 — 70B dense. No vision, no thinking. Sampling: T=0.6, top_p=0.9.

Microsoft phi-4 — 14B. Small + strong reasoning. NOTE: no tools capability upstream — combining with `ofa.enableTools=true` silently degrades to Ask-mode behavior (schema forwards, tool_calls never emitted).
IBM Granite 4 — 32B MoE (~19 GB active). Tools-capable. Sampling: T=0.7, top_p=0.95.
OpenAI gpt-oss — 116.8B MoE (~5B active), MXFP4, ~65 GB. Tools + thinking, no vision. Sourced from shared Kestrel install via symlink (no duplicate bytes). Sampling: T=1.0, top_p=1.0 per OpenAI card.
ofa.model.markdownDescription: LLM backing `ofa --serve`. Passed to bin/ofa as `OFA_MODEL` at spawn. Leave empty to inherit `bin/ofa`'s default. **Server-wide, not per-model-picker-entry**: all eight `OFA · *` entries in the Copilot Chat picker route to the SAME LLM selected here — they differ only in prompt + RAG. To change the LLM you must run **OFA: Disconnect** then **OFA: Connect** so a new `ofa --serve` process picks up the new value.

ofa.enableTools.markdownDescription: Pass `--serve-enable-tools` to `ofa --serve`. Enables tool-calling passthrough so VS Code Agent mode can chain file edits / terminal commands.

ofa.laptopSideBridgePort.markdownDescription: Login-node port for the `ssh -N -L` bridge that forwards `kestrel-login:PORT` → `<compute-node>:<remote-port>`. VS Code Remote-SSH auto-forwards this to `laptop:PORT`, so your laptop's `chatLanguageModels.json` can pin `http://localhost:PORT/v1/chat/completions` and stay valid across SLURM allocations. Match the port your existing `byok-update-config.py` setup used (default `49643`).

ofa.autoConnectOnStartup.markdownDescription: Automatically call `OFA: Connect` when a workspace opens on a detected Kestrel host. Opt-in because it burns a SLURM allocation whether you plan to use ofa or not.

ofa.healthProbeIntervalSeconds.markdownDescription: How often to poll `/healthz` on the ofa endpoint. First failed probe triggers the silent-reconnect flow.

ofa.silentReconnect.markdownDescription: When the SLURM job expires mid-session, silently re-allocate and rewire the endpoint (a toast notifies the user). Disable to be prompted before each re-allocate.

scripts.package: npm run build && npx --yes @vscode/vsce@^3 package --no-yarn --no-dependencies -o ofa-vscode.vsix
